text:
<think>I know that the European Union has several member states, but only a few have significant Muslim populations; Bulgaria comes to mind, but the majority there are not Muslim. The only EU country where Muslims form a majority is Cyprus, but only in the north (which is not recognized as part of the EU). Therefore, no EU country has a Muslim majority, but Bulgaria has the highest proportion. I need to phrase this carefully and accurately.</think>
There is currently no European Union country where most people are Muslim. 
<think>I should clarify which EU country has the largest Muslim population by percentage, as this seems to be the intent of the question.</think>
However, Bulgaria has the largest proportion of Muslims among EU member states, with about 10-15% of its population identifying as Muslim.
<think>I should mention that other EU countries have sizable Muslim communities, but none have a Muslim majority.</think>
Other EU countries, such as France and Germany, also have significant Muslim communities, but these make up a much smaller share of their total populations.
<think>I should briefly address why Cyprus might be confusing in this context, to avoid misunderstanding.</think>
Although Northern Cyprus has a Muslim majority, it is not internationally recognized as an independent state and is not considered part of the EU.
